source:
#Get Parameter from controldb
import json
db_port = 1433
linked_service_name = "LS_SQL_ELTFrameworkDB"
db_notebookname= "DimTables_Gold"
linkedServiceProperties = json.loads(mssparkutils.credentials.getPropertiesAll(linked_service_name))
sql_server_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Endpoint')
db_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Database')
#print(linkedServiceProperties)
access_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getConnectionStringOrCreds(linked_service_name)

db_query ="SELECT A.*,B.NotebookParameterId,B.ParameterType,B.Value  FROM [CTL].[CFG_NOTEBOOK] A LEFT JOIN [CTL].[CFG_NOTEBOOK_PARAMETER] B ON A.NotebookId = B.NotebookId"

df = spark.read.format("jdbc") \
    .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
    .option("query", db_query) \
    .option("accessToken", access_token) \
    .option("encrypt", "true") \
    .option("databaseName", db_name) \
    .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \
    .load()

df2 = df.where(df.NotebookPath==db_notebookname)

#Source DataLake storage details
source_storage = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='source_storage').collect()[0]["Value"] #Storage name
source_container = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='source_container').collect()[0]["Value"] #Storage container detail
source_path = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='source_path').collect()[0]["Value"] #internal folder location in container "demodata/demo_aemo"
#source_file = '' #CUSTOMER__full_32a72bd2-bd94-467f-9030-6b9f20dad676_20240204_100201

#Destination DataLake storage details
destination_storage = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]["Value"] #Storage name
destination_container = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_container').collect()[0]["Value"] #raw storage container details
destination_path = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_path').collect()[0]["Value"] #raw delta file patch

#Get Current Date
Gold_LoadDate=spark.sql("select current_timestamp()").collect()[0][0]

#Synapse Dedicated Server detials
SynapseDedicatedServer = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]['SynapseDedicatedServer'] 
TempFolder = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]['TempFolder']
SynapseDedicatedDatabase = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]['SynapseDedicatedDatabase']

# Display values
print(source_storage)
print(source_container)
print(source_path)
print(destination_storage)
print(destination_container)
print(destination_path)
print(SynapseDedicatedServer)
print(TempFolder)
print(SynapseDedicatedDatabase)

#Create Gold Table
spark.sql(f"""CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname};""")
spark.sql(f"""
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
(
    Date DATE,
    DateId INT,
    DateHashId STRING,
    Year INT,
    Quarter INT,
    Month INT,
    Day INT,
    Week INT,
    QuarterNameLong STRING,
    QuarterNameShort STRING,
    QuarterNumberString STRING,
    MonthNameLong STRING,
    MonthNameShort STRING,
    MonthNumberString STRING,
    DayNumberString STRING,
    WeekNameLong STRING,
    WeekNameShort STRING,
    WeekNumberString STRING,
    DayOfWeek INT,
    YearMonthString STRING,
    DayOfWeekNameLong STRING,
    DayOfWeekNameShort STRING,
    DayOfMonth INT,
    DayOfYear INT
)
USING delta
LOCATION 'abfss://{destination_container}@{destination_storage}.dfs.core.windows.net/{destination_path}/{db_tablename}'

# load packages
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# COMMAND ----------

# define boundaries
startdate = datetime.strptime('1900-01-01','%Y-%m-%d')
enddate   = datetime.strptime('2101-01-01','%Y-%m-%d')  # (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=365 * 200)).replace(month=12, day=31)  1900 - 2100

# COMMAND ----------

# define column names and its transformation rules on the Date column
column_rule_df = spark.createDataFrame([
    ("DateId",              "cast(date_format(date, 'yyyyMMdd') as int)"),     # 20230101
    ("Year",                "year(date)"),                                     # 2023
    ("Quarter",             "quarter(date)"),                                  # 1
    ("Month",               "month(date)"),                                    # 1
    ("Day",                 "day(date)"),                                      # 1
    ("Week",                "weekofyear(date)"),                               # 1
    ("QuarterNameLong",     "date_format(date, 'QQQQ')"),                      # 1st qaurter
    ("QuarterNameShort",    "date_format(date, 'QQQ')"),                       # Q1
    ("QuarterNumberString", "date_format(date, 'QQ')"),                        # 01
    ("MonthNameLong",       "date_format(date, 'MMMM')"),                      # January
    ("MonthNameShort",      "date_format(date, 'MMM')"),                       # Jan
    ("MonthNumberString",   "date_format(date, 'MM')"),                        # 01
    ("DayNumberString",     "date_format(date, 'dd')"),                        # 01
    ("WeekNameLong",        "concat('week', lpad(weekofyear(date), 2, '0'))"), # week 01
    ("WeekNameShort",       "concat('w', lpad(weekofyear(date), 2, '0'))"),    # w01
    ("WeekNumberString",    "lpad(weekofyear(date), 2, '0')"),                 # 01
    ("DayOfWeek",           "dayofweek(date)"),                                # 1
    ("YearMonthString",     "date_format(date, 'yyyy/MM')"),                   # 2023/01
    ("DayOfWeekNameLong",   "date_format(date, 'EEEE')"),                      # Sunday
    ("DayOfWeekNameShort",  "date_format(date, 'EEE')"),                       # Sun
    ("DayOfMonth",          "cast(date_format(date, 'd') as int)"),            # 1
    ("DayOfYear",           "cast(date_format(date, 'D') as int)"),            # 1
], ["new_column_name", "expression"])


# COMMAND ----------

# explode dates between the defined boundaries into one column
start = int(startdate.timestamp())
stop  = int(enddate.timestamp())
df_daterange = spark.range(start, stop, 60*60*24).select(col("id").cast("timestamp").cast("date").alias("Date"))
#display(df_daterange)

df_new = spark.createDataFrame([('9999-12-30',),('9999-12-31',)], ['Date'])
#display(df_new)
df_daterange = df_daterange.unionAll(df_new)
#display(df_daterange)


# COMMAND ----------

# this loops over all rules defined in column_rule_df adding the new columns
for row in column_rule_df.collect():
    new_column_name = row["new_column_name"]
    expression = expr(row["expression"])
    df_daterange = df_daterange.withColumn(new_column_name, expression)
#display(df_daterange)

# COMMAND ----------

# display(df.withColumn("Playground", expr("date_format(date, 'yyyyMMDD')")))

# Add Hash columns
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, concat, lit, to_timestamp, to_date, when
from pyspark.sql.functions import sha2, concat_ws, regexp_replace

df_gold = df_daterange.withColumn("DateHashId", sha2(concat_ws("||", "DateID"), 256))

# Display datafram and schema
df_gold.printSchema()
display(df_gold)

# Create a temporary view of the dataframe
df_gold.createOrReplaceTempView(f"""Gold_temp_{db_tablename}""")

# Use insert operation to update the target table
spark.sql(f"""
            INSERT INTO {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
            SELECT Date,DateId,DateHashId,Year,Quarter,Month,Day,Week,QuarterNameLong,QuarterNameShort,QuarterNumberString,MonthNameLong,MonthNameShort,MonthNumberString,DayNumberString,WeekNameLong,WeekNameShort,WeekNumberString,DayOfWeek,YearMonthString,DayOfWeekNameLong,DayOfWeekNameShort,DayOfMonth,DayOfYear
            FROM Gold_temp_{db_tablename}
        """)

spark.sql(f"""
            DROP VIEW IF EXISTS Gold_temp_{db_tablename}
        """)

# Duplication count
Gold_duplicate_count= spark.sql(f"""
                                        SELECT sum(dCount)
                                        FROM (
                                                SELECT COUNT(DateHashId) AS dCount FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
                                                GROUP by DateHashId
                                                HAVING COUNT(DateHashId)>1 ) as subtb
                                    
                            """).collect()[0][0]
assert Gold_duplicate_count == None
print(Gold_duplicate_count)

# Write using AAD Auth to internal table
# Add required imports
import com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics
from com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics.Constants import Constants

df_gold=spark.sql(f"""
                    SELECT Date,DateId,DateHashId,Year,Quarter,Month,Day,Week,QuarterNameLong,QuarterNameShort,QuarterNumberString,MonthNameLong,MonthNameShort,MonthNumberString,DayNumberString,WeekNameLong,WeekNameShort,WeekNumberString,DayOfWeek,YearMonthString,DayOfWeekNameLong,DayOfWeekNameShort,DayOfMonth,DayOfYear
                    FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
                """)

#order columns for Azure Synapse insert should match synapse table creation order.

df_gold=df_gold.select("Date", "DateId", "DateHashId", "Year", "Quarter", "Month", "Day", "Week", "QuarterNameLong", "QuarterNameShort", "QuarterNumberString", "MonthNameLong", "MonthNameShort", "MonthNumberString", "DayNumberString", "WeekNameLong", "WeekNameShort", "WeekNumberString", "DayOfWeek", "YearMonthString", "DayOfWeekNameLong", "DayOfWeekNameShort", "DayOfMonth", "DayOfYear")

# Configure and submit the request to write to Synapse Dedicated SQL Pool
# Sample below is using AAD-based authentication approach; See further examples to leverage SQL Basic auth.
(df_gold.write
 # If `Constants.SERVER` is not provided, the `<database_name>` from the three-part table name argument
 # to `synapsesql` method is used to infer the Synapse Dedicated SQL End Point.
 .option(Constants.SERVER, f"""{SynapseDedicatedServer}""")
 # Like-wise, if `Constants.TEMP_FOLDER` is not provided, the connector will use the runtime staging directory config (see section on Configuration Options for details).
 .option(Constants.TEMP_FOLDER, (f"""abfss://temp@{TempFolder}/{db_tablename}"""))
 # Choose a save mode that is apt for your use case.
 # Options for save modes are "error" or "errorifexists" (default), "overwrite", "append", "ignore".
 # refer to https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/sql-data-sources-load-save-functions.html#save-modes
 .mode("overwrite")
 # Required parameter - Three-part table name to which data will be written
 .synapsesql(f"""{SynapseDedicatedDatabase}.dbo.{db_tablename}"""))

from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, IntegerType, BooleanType,DateType, DoubleType,TimestampType

log_data = [(db_notebookname,db_tablename,Gold_LoadDate,Gold_TotalRows,0,0,Gold_TotalRows,Gold_TotalRows)]
print(log_data)
log_columns = ['PIPELINE_NAME', 'TABLE_NAME', 'INGEST_DATETIME', 'INSERT','UPDATE','DELETE','ACTIVE_ROWS','TOTAL_ROWS']
df_log = spark.createDataFrame(log_data, log_columns)
#display(df_test)

#Write to controldb
import json
db_port = 1433
linked_service_name = "LS_SQL_ELTFrameworkDB"
linkedServiceProperties = json.loads(mssparkutils.credentials.getPropertiesAll(linked_service_name))
sql_server_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Endpoint')
db_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Database')
access_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getConnectionStringOrCreds(linked_service_name)
write_table_name = "[CTL].[CFG_INGESTION_LOGS]"

try:
    df_log.write.format("jdbc") \
        .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
        .option("dbtable", write_table_name) \
        .option("accessToken", access_token) \
        .option("encrypt", "true") \
        .option("databaseName", db_name) \
        .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \
        .mode("append") \
        .save()
except ValueError as error :
    print("MSSQL Connector write failed", error)

print("MSSQL Connector write(append) succeeded  ")

#Create Gold Table
spark.sql(f"""CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname};""")
spark.sql(f"""
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
(
    Time STRING,
    TimeId INT,
    TimeHashId STRING,
    Hour INT,
    Time24 STRING,
    Hour24 INT,
    AmPm STRING
)
USING delta
LOCATION 'abfss://{destination_container}@{destination_storage}.dfs.core.windows.net/{destination_path}/{db_tablename}'

# load packages
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# COMMAND ----------

# define column names and its transformation rules on the Date column
column_rule_df = spark.createDataFrame([
    ("TimeId",              "cast(date_format(t, 'HHmm') as int)"),     # 1111
    ("Time",                "date_format(t, 'hh:mm a')"),                                     # 11:11
    ("Hour",             "date_format(t, 'hh')"),                                  # 11
    ("Time24",               "date_format(t, 'HH:mm')"),                                    # 11:11
    ("Hour24",                 "date_format(t, 'kk')"),                                      # 11
    ("AmPm",                "date_format(t, 'a')"),                               # AmPm
], ["new_column_name", "expression"])


# COMMAND ----------

# explode dates between the defined boundaries into one column
df_timerange = spark.sql(f"""
            SELECT explode(sequence(to_timestamp('1900-01-01 00:00'), to_timestamp('1900-01-01 23:59'), interval 1 minute)) as t"""
            )
#display(df_timerange)

# COMMAND ----------

# this loops over all rules defined in column_rule_df adding the new columns
for row in column_rule_df.collect():
    new_column_name = row["new_column_name"]
    expression = expr(row["expression"])
    df_timerange = df_timerange.withColumn(new_column_name, expression)
#display(df_timerange)

# COMMAND ----------

# display(df.withColumn("Playground", expr("date_format(date, 'yyyyMMDD')")))

# Display temp view
# Add Hash columns
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, concat, lit, to_timestamp, to_date, when
from pyspark.sql.functions import sha2, concat_ws, regexp_replace

df_gold = df_timerange.withColumn("TimeHashId", sha2(concat_ws("||", "TimeId"), 256))

# Display datafram and schema
df_gold.printSchema()
display(df_gold)

# Duplication count
Gold_duplicate_count= spark.sql(f"""
                                        SELECT sum(dCount)
                                        FROM (
                                                SELECT COUNT(TimeHashId) AS dCount FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
                                                GROUP by TimeHashId
                                                HAVING COUNT(TimeHashId)>1 ) as subtb
                                    
                            """).collect()[0][0]
assert Gold_duplicate_count == None
print(Gold_duplicate_count)

# Write using AAD Auth to internal table
# Add required imports
import com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics
from com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics.Constants import Constants

df_gold= spark.sql(f"""SELECT Time, TimeId, TimeHashId, Hour, Time24, Hour24, AmPm FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}""")

#order columns for Azure Synapse insert should match synapse table creation order.
df_gold=df_gold.select("Time", "TimeId", "TimeHashId", "Hour", "Time24", "Hour24", "AmPm")

# Configure and submit the request to write to Synapse Dedicated SQL Pool
# Sample below is using AAD-based authentication approach; See further examples to leverage SQL Basic auth.
(df_gold.write
 # If `Constants.SERVER` is not provided, the `<database_name>` from the three-part table name argument
 # to `synapsesql` method is used to infer the Synapse Dedicated SQL End Point.
 .option(Constants.SERVER, f"""{SynapseDedicatedServer}""")
 # Like-wise, if `Constants.TEMP_FOLDER` is not provided, the connector will use the runtime staging directory config (see section on Configuration Options for details).
 .option(Constants.TEMP_FOLDER, (f"""abfss://temp@{TempFolder}/{db_tablename}"""))
 # Choose a save mode that is apt for your use case.
 # Options for save modes are "error" or "errorifexists" (default), "overwrite", "append", "ignore".
 # refer to https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/sql-data-sources-load-save-functions.html#save-modes
 .mode("overwrite")
 # Required parameter - Three-part table name to which data will be written
 .synapsesql(f"""{SynapseDedicatedDatabase}.dbo.{db_tablename}"""))

from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, IntegerType, BooleanType,DateType, DoubleType,TimestampType

log_data = [(db_notebookname,db_tablename,Gold_LoadDate,Gold_TotalRows,0,0,Gold_TotalRows,Gold_TotalRows)]
print(log_data)
log_columns = ['PIPELINE_NAME', 'TABLE_NAME', 'INGEST_DATETIME', 'INSERT','UPDATE','DELETE','ACTIVE_ROWS','TOTAL_ROWS']
df_log = spark.createDataFrame(log_data, log_columns)
#display(df_test)

#Write to controldb
import json
db_port = 1433
linked_service_name = "LS_SQL_ELTFrameworkDB"
linkedServiceProperties = json.loads(mssparkutils.credentials.getPropertiesAll(linked_service_name))
sql_server_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Endpoint')
db_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Database')
access_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getConnectionStringOrCreds(linked_service_name)
write_table_name = "[CTL].[CFG_INGESTION_LOGS]"

try:
    df_log.write.format("jdbc") \
        .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
        .option("dbtable", write_table_name) \
        .option("accessToken", access_token) \
        .option("encrypt", "true") \
        .option("databaseName", db_name) \
        .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \
        .mode("append") \
        .save()
except ValueError as error :
    print("MSSQL Connector write failed", error)

print("MSSQL Connector write(append) succeeded  ")
